summary: tests/common/mod.rs:58 spawns bare `npm` (NotFound on Windows); reuse install.rs's npm_program() instead of re-duplicating the Windows/npm.cmd conditional

edit.description: Make `npm_program() -> &'static str` (line 1311, already picks `npm.cmd` on Windows / `npm` elsewhere) `pub` — `install` is already a `pub mod` in lib.rs, so this is the only visibility change needed.

`ensure_sdk_built()` (line 58) calls `std::process::Command::new("npm")` directly — `Command`'s launch doesn't consult PATHEXT, so this is `NotFound` on every Windows box. Replace the literal with `std::process::Command::new(temper::install::npm_program())`.

acceptance: tests/common/mod.rs no longer spawns a bare `"npm"` literal; it calls the now-`pub` `temper::install::npm_program()`; `cargo test` still builds and runs the SDK build step unchanged on non-Windows (npm_program() returns "npm" there, so behavior is byte-identical off Windows).
notes: Windows field report (chore 352477d, observed at 2285821). Confirmed on disk: src/install.rs:1311 npm_program() is private; tests/common/mod.rs:58 is the only bare Command::new("npm") in src/ or tests/ (grep-verified). Zero behavior change on non-Windows. Re-verified live at d7e35f6. scoped at d7e35f6

summary: Add .gitattributes pinning byte-fidelity fixtures (builtin_lock.toml, .snap files, tests/fixtures/**) to LF so Windows autocrlf clones don't false-fail strict byte-equality tests

new.description: Pin the byte-fidelity fixture set to `text eol=lf` so checkout never rewrites them regardless of a clone's `core.autocrlf`: `src/builtin_lock.toml` (the embedded built-in lock, byte-compared in tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs against a fresh SDK emit), `tests/snapshots/*.snap` (20 insta snapshots), and `tests/fixtures/**` (the rules/extract_equivalence/coordinate fixture trees consumed by strict-equality round-trip tests — all confirmed plain text, none binary).

acceptance: `.gitattributes` exists at repo root; `git check-attr eol -- src/builtin_lock.toml tests/snapshots/<any>.snap tests/fixtures/rules/clean` each report `eol: lf`; the byte-fidelity fixture set is no longer subject to checkout-time EOL conversion on any clone.
notes: Windows field report (chore 352477d, observed at 2285821). Confirmed: no .gitattributes exists; 20 tests/snapshots/*.snap and 11 tests/fixtures/** files, all plain text, counts re-verified unchanged. Fence widened to .gitattributes at d7e35f6 (was the sole revert cause). Broader byte-fidelity audit beyond this listed set is optional follow-on, not this entry's scope. scoped at d7e35f6

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at d7e35f6: still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
